subject.title: Mathematics Analysis and Approaches (AA)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics Analysis and Approaches** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **number and algebra, functions, geometry and trigonometry, statistics and probability, and calculus**, with a strong focus on **theoretical understanding, formal mathematical reasoning, and analytical problem-solving**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Non-calculator written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 2** | Calculator allowed written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 3** | Calculator allowed problem-solving | N/A | 60 minutes | 20% (HL only) |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal Assessment | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: Clear table of contents and numbered headings providing a roadmap
Logical progression of sections with cross-referenced equations
Embedded figures, tables and equations in relevant places
weaknesses: Occasional structural drift (e.g. misplaced HL header, disconnected appendix)
Lengthy narrative digressions and repeated background reduce conciseness
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments place the work solidly in band 3 for coherence and organization, with clear logical development and structure. However, repeated background material and occasional digressions prevent it from reaching the highest level of conciseness and seamless flow. A score of 3 reflects good organization and logical progression with room to trim redundant content.

strengths: Consistent definition and use of standard symbols (S, Î±, Ï, C, T)
Effective use of multiple representations (equations, tables, graphs, schematics)
Stepâbyâstep derivations and logical linking of maths to physical meaning
weaknesses: Notation inconsistencies (e.g. Tâ² vs. Tâ´, mixed HTML subscripts) and occasional undefined variables
Formatting glitches in equation numbering and symbol table ambiguities
Lowâresolution figures and inconsistent display of equations
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The communication is clear and logically structured, with mostly consistent mathematical language and a variety of effective representations. Minor lapses in notation, formatting and figure clarity prevent a topâband 4 but secure a solid band 3 performance overall.

strengths: Evidence of independent thinking in deriving models from first principles
Personal context (Malaysia, aspiring chemical engineer) grounds the exploration
Initiative shown by extending standard models with a greenhouse function
weaknesses: Approach remains within conventional energyâbalance literature methods
Personal narrative, while clear, does not reach highly innovative depth
Creativity and initiative are good but not sustained at an exceptional level
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student demonstrates significant independent thinking and a clear personal approach, with good creativity in model extension. These qualities align with band 2, as the work goes beyond replication but not to an outstanding level of innovation.

strengths: Meaningful reflection on key assumptions and their limitations
Thoughtful analysis of discrepancies between modelled and observed temperature changes
Clear evaluation of outcome implications and suggestions for future improvements
weaknesses: Reflection remains at a surface level without deep critical exploration
Limited quantitative evaluation of model errors or alternative formulations
Future improvement suggestions are not elaborated with rigorous plans
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The reflection shows consistent and thoughtful analysis of assumptions, results and outcomes, touching on limitations and improvements. The work fits band 2, as it lacks the depth and sustained critical insight required for topâband performance.

strengths: Solid use of differentiation, integration factors and nonâlinear ODEs
Accurate application of the StefanâBoltzmann law and exponential integration
Mathematics fully commensurate with SL course level and directly relevant to the climate model
weaknesses: Minor algebraic and sign errors in derivative steps
Incorrect attribution of terrestrial radiation source and excessive significant figures in some calcuations
Abrupt introduction of variables without full definitions
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The mathematics is appropriate, comprehensive and accurately applied at SL level, showing good understanding. Minor errors and inconsistencies prevent the topmost band, placing the work securely in band 4.

strandSelections.reasoning: Most paragraphs follow a logical mathematical progression; derivations are shown step-by-step and linked to physical meaning.  Occasional formatting glitches and lengthy digressions cloud clarity but overall communication is good and understandable.

Derivations are largely correct and show good conceptual grasp; minor algebraic slips (e.g., P = ÏTâ², notation inconsistencies) and an over-estimated temperature rise indicate that understanding is not flawless, so âmostly correct and good understandingâ is appropriate.

The student employs equations, tables, two line graphs and several schematic diagrams to support explanations.  Graphs are simple but correctly labelled; tables list data and percentage change.  The variety is effective, yet scale/clarity issues (very small images) limit the work from being âhighly effectiveâ.

The exploration proceeds in a mostly logical sequence: simple energy balance model â atmospheric extension â carbon-cycle ODE â coupled model â parameter sensitivity.  Each new section builds on the previous mathematics; cross-referencing of equations (e.g., (1), (2), (25)) shows conscious linking.  However, the thread is occasionally interrupted by lengthy narrative paragraphs and duplicated background that detract from a fully seamless flow, preventing the âexcellentâ descriptor.

Creating a coupled climate-carbon model and performing parameter sensitivity shows creativity and initiative beyond a single ready-made model.  The work is solid but not exceptional in novelty, placing it in the âgoodâ band.

Comparisons between modelled 50 K rise and observed 2 K rise show thoughtful analysis of validity.  Still, the critique remains surface-level and does not explore alternative formulations or quantitative error estimates, so insight is âthoughtfulâ rather than âdeep and insightfulâ.

Outcomes of parameter tests are evaluated (solar/albedo minor, COâ model inaccurate) and linked to model usefulness.  Future improvements are suggested but not elaborated with rigorous plans, giving a clear yet not comprehensive evaluation.

The student reflects on assumptions (spherical Earth, constant emissions) and recognises how they limit realism, indicating meaningful reflection throughout.  The discussion is present but not deeply sustained, fitting the middle band.

Standard symbols (S, Î±, Ï, C, T) are defined in a table and used consistently in subsequent formulae; calculus notation (dT/dt, â«, e^{kt}) is mostly correct.  Minor lapses such as Tâ for Tâ´ and mixed HTML subscripts show that usage is not flawless, but it is generally appropriate and clear.

The student frames the investigation around personal experience in Malaysia and selects parameters to vary; this demonstrates a clear personal approach.  The engagement, however, remains conventional and does not reach a âhighly personal and innovativeâ level.

While most material is related to the modelling aim, the write-up is extremely long and often repeats basic physical explanations (e.g., multiple derivations of surface area, repeated definitions of Ï).  Personal narrative paragraphs and extensive quotations reduce conciseness.  Thus content is âmostly relevantâ but not consistently concise.

All mathematics directly addresses climate-temperature relationships: Stefan-Boltzmann derivation, ODEs for heat balance and COâ cycle, sensitivity calculations.  The selection is relevant and extends across the exploration effectively.

The choice to derive models from first principles and to test sensitivity reveals noticeable independent thinking beyond textbook replication.  Nonetheless, methods are standard to energy-balance literature and heavily supported by cited sources, so evidence is âsignificantâ rather than âoutstandingâ.

A clear table of contents, numbered headings, and sub-sections (3.1, 3.2 â¦ 6) give the reader an explicit roadmap.  Figures, tables and equations are embedded in the relevant places.  The structure occasionally drifts (e.g., HL header appears mid-discussion; appendix table is disconnected), so the organisation is good rather than exceptional.

Differentiation, integration factors, logarithmic forcing and non-linear ODEs are solidly within HL level.  The mathematics is fully commensurate with the course but not unusually sophisticated, aligning with band 4.

annotations.comment: The introduction contains extended general background and repeated descriptions, which reduces conciseness. Focus on directly linking narrative to the mathematical aims to improve relevance and trim unnecessary content.
The studentâs personal motivation (âaspiring chemical engineerâ and context of Malaysia) effectively demonstrates personal engagement and grounds the exploration in genuine interest.
The symbol table uses âSâ for both solar constant and surface area, creating ambiguity. Use distinct notation (e.g. A for area) to avoid confusion.
Equation (1) label is placed incorrectly in the numerator line. Use \tag{1} or place the reference consistently to maintain clarity.
Figure 1 is very small and lowâresolution, making it difficult to see details. Increase its size or resolution for better readability.
The notation switches between S and I for solar constant without explanation. Define each variable clearly before use to ensure consistent mathematical communication.
Intermediate results in the StefanâBoltzmann calculation retain excessive significant figures. Round appropriately to reflect measurement precision.
The derivation of S includes division by 4 from geometry but lacks a concise geometric explanation. Consider adding a brief diagram or step to clarify why area averages over 4ÏRÂ².
Constant k is introduced in P = kTâ´ without prior definition or physical meaning. Define k (e.g. k = ÎµÏ) before using it for clarity.

The description attributes terrestrial infrared radiation to fission of radioactive elements, which is incorrect. Terrestrial radiation stems from the Earthâs thermal emission; revise this explanation.
The simplification Energy In = S(1âÎ±)/4 builds logically on previous steps, showing coherent development of the modelâs structure.
Application of the StefanâBoltzmann law as Energy Out = ÏTâ´ is accurate and demonstrates correct use of mathematical formalisms for radiative loss.
Variable w appears without an explicit definition at its first use. Introduce and define w = 1âÎ± before employing it in equations.
The derivative \(dT_e/dS\) is derived incorrectly. The exponent rules are misapplied; revisit differentiation of \((Sw/4Ï)^{1/4}\).
The discussion paragraph is lengthy and digresses from core derivations. Condense narrative to maintain logical mathematical development.
Equation label appears as âEnergy ln â Energy Outâ. The typo âlnâ should read âInâ. Correct the text inside the equation.
The combined derivative expression for \(d^2T/dt^2\) is correct but lacks parentheses around numerator and denominator. Add them to improve readability.
The chain rule application in differentiating \(ÎµÏT^4\) misses the factor 4 correctly and shows a sign error. Re-examine this step for accuracy.
The interpretation of the second derivativeâs dependence on heat capacity and \(T^3\) is insightful, showing critical understanding of the modelâs transient behavior.
The integration back to the exponential form is thorough and shows solid manipulation of logarithmic integration. Well demonstrated.
The atmospheric model section begins abruptly without defining \(T_0(t)\) and \(T_1(t)\) clearly in context. Introduce these variables with full physical description.
Figure 4 effectively illustrates the twoâlayer atmosphere model, aiding mathematical communication through clear visual representation.
Notation \(\tilde T_0\) and \(\check T_1\) appear later without introduction of the symbols; unify notation and define them explicitly.
Equation (13) is embedded in text rather than displayed with \tag formatting. Use consistent displayed equation numbering for clarity.
Introducing the greenhouse function \(G(\epsilon,p)\) shows initiative in extending the model beyond standard formulation.
